entry.title: 🚀 SAP RAP Events: Custom Logic Without BAdIs or User Exits
entry.content:
As SAP S/4HANA continues to evolve with clean core principles, the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) is taking center stage. One of the most underused but powerful features of RAP is its event-based architecture, which allows you to plug in custom logic in a clean and upgrade-safe way.

Today, I want to share how you can listen to standard RAP events and trigger your own custom events — no BAdIs or user exits needed!

🧠 The Concept: RAP Event Listeners

In classic SAP ABAP, you’d typically write enhancements using:

  • 🔧 BAdIs

  • 🔄 User exits

  • 💡 Implicit enhancements

But in RAP, you can react to standard business behavior via events. That means you can write your own custom logic that responds when something happens in a standard RAP BO (Business Object) — like a purchase order being approved — by listening to its published events.

This is cleaner, future-proof, and doesn't modify the standard codebase.

🎯 Real Use Case

Let’s say SAP’s standard BO for PurchaseOrder publishes an event when a PO is approved. You want to send a custom email, update another Z table, or trigger integration — only when that event happens.

✅ In RAP, you can do this like so:

  • Step 1: The standard RAP object publishes the approval event (R_PurchaseOrderTP)

  • Step 2: Your custom RAP behavior definition listens for that event.

  • Step 3: You trigger a custom implementation, e.g., a mail sender class or integration call.

No BAdI. No user exit. No modifications.

🖼️ Illustration: RAP Event Flow

Figure 1: Listening to Standard RAP Events in a Custom BO

🛠️ How to Implement

Here’s a simplified code sketch of how this works:

1. Listen to Standard Event in Custom BO

CLASS zcl_po_ext DEFINITION

PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL

FOR EVENTS OF R_PurchaseOrderTP.

PUBLIC SECTION.

PROTECTED SECTION.

PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

2. Handle the Event in Your Class

CLASS lhe_po_ext DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

PRIVATE SECTION.

METHODS on_approved FOR ENTITY EVENT

approved FOR purchaseorder~approved.

ENDCLASS.

You’re done — and you didn’t need to touch a single line of standard code!

💡 Why This Matters

  • 🔒 Clean Core Compliant – Stay upgrade-safe.

  • ⚡ No Enhancements or BAdIs Needed – Simpler architecture.

  • 🧱 Composable – Build logic modularly using RAP principles.

  • 🔁 Reusability – Listen to multiple events, reuse handlers across objects.

📣 Final Thoughts

The RAP event mechanism offers a paradigm shift in how we build custom logic in SAP. It allows us to be event-driven, clean, and compliant with SAP's future direction.

If you’re developing on S/4HANA or looking to modernize your ABAP stack — RAP events are your secret weapon.

👀 Have you tried this approach in your projects? I’d love to hear your experience. Let’s connect and share ideas!

🔗 #SAPRAP #CleanCore #S4HANA #ABAP #SAPDevelopers #NoMoreBADIs #EventDrivenArchitecture

entry.title: Re: Unable to find deposit payment processing capability...
entry.content:
In SAP there is a standard contract type DO01 and DO02 that is specifically used for a "Security Deposit Contract". The disadvantage of this is that you end of with 2 contracts, one for the security deposit and one for the actual lease-out, so I generally don't like this approach.

The Bond scenario will have to be configured if you want to use it only on the lease-out. The quick way is to copy the rent condition type and re-label this as "Bond" and use the same rent flow type or create new flow types. This would be the approach for a demo only.

For a real implementation you must check with the customer what the expectation is regarding journal entries. Bond is generally not captured as a revenue entry so therefor you'll have to check what account is required and also if this should be posted to the default customer recon account or if this requires a special GL indicator to post it to a separate customer recon account to capture "RE Rent Deposits or Bonds".

The second complexity is at the end of the lease. How should the original bond be treated as there are 3 scenarios:

  1. Full refund
  2. Partial refund
  3. No refund

There are different ways to achieve this, but probably the simplest way would be to set the original bond condition type to zero at the end of the lease which will trigger a reversal of the original entry. If any money is to be retained a one-time rent or separate condition type (e.g. Bond Retained) can be added to the contract.

You'll end up with 2 cashflows, one for the full bond return and one for the amount that is to be retained.

There are variations of this setup so it is important to discuss this with the customer.

entry.title: Re: Credit exposure commitment type 500 is not updated wh...
entry.content:
It could be due to

  • billing document created from FWSD might not be triggering the standard credit exposure update logic due to missing integration points
  • Missing or Inactive BAdIs like BADI_SD_CM or UKM_R3_ACTIVATE since Credit Management relies on these
  • Queue Issues which can be checked in SMQ1 for stuck queues like UKM_DOCUMENT_PROCESS

If required, you can execute transactions like UKM_COMMITMENTS, UKM_TRANSFER_ITEMS, or UKM_RVKRED07 to rebuild the credit exposure manually

entry.title: Re: Auto Import to PRE-PRD - in ChaRM
entry.content: Hi Swu, Thank you for the details. Can you also let me know if there are any disadvantages if we schedule the auto import when the status changed to import to PRE-PRD. I want to understand the Pro's and Con's before configuring the auto import for status to Pre-PRD. Please help to provide Pro's and Cons.

entry.title: Want to Process the Sales orders against the particular MRP only
entry.content:
Currently, during Sales Order (SO) creation in SAP, the system automatically selects the first available MRP (based on date/month) and deducts quantity from it. This leads to incorrect stock consumption and affects Raw Material (RM) planning.

We require the ability to manually select the MRP record (product-wise, month-wise, or project-wise) during SO creation. The system should then consume quantity only from the selected MRP and not affect any other MRP record. This is necessary to ensure accurate RM requirement planning and avoid consumption mismatches.

entry.title: Re: Fiori MYINBOX PO no details
entry.content: We have exactly the same issue after SAP patch. Any solution provided by SAP?

entry.title: Kindly help with standard report name in SAP Public cloud for Int. rate used in the Money Market.
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I would appreciate your assistance in identifying the standard report name in SAP Public Cloud (Treasury and Risk Management) that displays the interest rate used in Money Market transactions.

For instance, when a financial transaction is created using Product Type 51A and Transaction Type 100, and a variable interest rate is applied to the deal, we need a standard report that can show the interest rate details used in that transaction.

Could you please guide us on the appropriate report name

Regards

Sourav Saha Bhowmick

entry.title: any standard API to export invoice attachment from SAP system and send to external service provider
entry.content:
any standard API to export invoice attachment from SAP system and send to external service provider?

In concur system -

The VAT IT App is an “standard offering” by SAP Concur and VAT IT (= SAP Concur Partner) and this app is offered in the App Center within Concur T&E (CTE). This is what we have enabled in CTE for SAP Concur system.

As this VAT IT App/the API is used to connect to the CTE system/tenant (to read data of SAP’s employees from the CTE databases), we would like same functionality / this API to pull data from other SAP system (Non Concur system)?

entry.title: Re: Auto Import to PRE-PRD - in ChaRM
entry.content: Hi Satish, as said in previous reply, in standard, we support auto-import if you use urgent change or normal change preliminary import, there are no explicit Pro's or Con's of auto-import, in standard, if you use urgent change or preliminary import, usually it means you want to import the TR as quick as possible, so it will trigger the auto-import during status change. This is standard supported scenario. Hope these help.

entry.title: Re: Become Part of the SAP TechEd Family as a Community Talk Speaker!
entry.content: Hi @foessleitner - the track depends on what your specific content is related to. If it's ABAP Cloud in SAP Build (building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP), then you should select AI-driven application development and extensibility with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build in the App Dev & Automation track. If it's more related to in-app/on-stack extensibility, I would recommend selected the Digital Transformation track and subtrack Extend and continuously innovate with cloud ERP.

entry.title: Re: Want to Process the Sales orders against the particul...
entry.content: Probably, you need to discuss with your PP team to address this MRP requirement.   Since you have not explained what is your current set up, it is not clear how the sale order creation impacts the MRP function.

entry.title: Re: Business Partner Grouping Control based on Role
entry.content:
Hi Andi,

I've implemented the code almost identically, but in the grouping dropdown list, the values relevant for the BP role are not appearing. I've already checked the customizing table, and everything seems to be configured correctly.

Do you have any idea where the issue might stil lie?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single...
entry.content:
To handle split extrainfo in Sybase ASE audit logs:
1. Use auditprocess utility or sp_audit parsing scripts to reconstruct events—they handle multi-row extrainfo properly.
2. There’s no supported way to increase extrainfo column length in sysaudits_xx tables.
3. To combine rows manually, use event, eventtime, and objname as correlation keys and STRING_AGG() (if on ASE 16+) or cursor logic in a stored proc.
4. For robust analysis, export logs and process externally (e.g., Python script or shell pipeline).
5. Consider using audit init with larger audit block sizes to reduce row-splitting frequency.

entry.title: Re: UI Generation in SAP custom business object app S/4 HANA On-Premise
entry.content: Still no option available for on prem to populate your custom fields without a developer?

entry.title: Re: Getting short dump FM BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS (Set/Reset Status for BUS2002 (Network))
entry.content: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT due to CALL SCREEN in function module CO_KO_ORDER_ENTRY_EXT. So, it's RAP but it's not detected as RAP. If it was detected as RAP, there would be no CALL SCREEN. Maybe you forget to call something like BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION or call it at the wrong moment.

entry.title: Enhance Central Finance error handling with AIF & ISM integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital landscape, minimizing downtime and streamlining document posting issues and their resolutions are crucial, especially for financial systems. Central Finance (CFIN) has already revolutionized finance operations, but what happens when document replication fails?

Enter the powerful integration of AIF (Application Interface Framework) and ISM (Incident Solution Matching), designed to tackle error handling with automation and intelligence.

Introducing SAP Note 3531918

SAP Note 3531918 unveils a new technique: integrating AIF with ISM for CFIN error handling. This update automates solution identification, enhancing issue resolution efficiency.

What’s new?

The functionality is accessible via the 'Functions' option within the AIF Monitor transaction that applies to each error message.

The new integration seamlessly connects AIF which manages business interface errors in Central Finance with ISM, an AI-powered service/API that matches incidents with relevant SAP solutions in the form of SAP Note, KBA, FAQ Notes etc.

Note:
This enhancement builds upon the framework introduced in SAP-Note 3389718, “Navigating AIF Errors: Central Finance Error Support and Solutions,” and SAP Note 3271558, “Central Finance: Enable AIF Error Resolution Support Content.” It primarily leverages the customization table V_CFIN_AIFKBAURL.

With this enhancement, the solution is now empowered by the ISM API, which utilizes Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) algorithms for context-based intelligent search, enabling a more dynamic AIF error resolution experience.

Key Benefits:

  • Automated Error Diagnosis - Error details are directly pushed from customer ‘s system to the ISM engine, eliminating guesswork.
  • Instant Solution Suggestions - ISM scans SAP’s knowledge base, providing context-specific resolutions automatically.
  • Reduced Manual Work - Users no longer need to search SAP Notes manually. They receive solutions within the CFIN error framework.

How does it work?

Think of it as having an SAP expert on-call 24/7.

Open the /N/AIF/ERR Transaction and click on the function option next to an error message in AIF and choose Add Incident Solution Matching Results.

The system packages technical metadata (like product component, system type, error description) and routes it to ISM via a secure HTTP connection. ISM then searches SAP’s database using NLP and ML, returning tailored recommendations.

The results are added to the Support Information section.

Note: As it is a context-based solution extraction, some of the recommended SAP Notes or suggestions may not be applicable to every customer scenario. It is at the customer's discretion to review, validate, and apply the appropriate solutions as per their system landscape and business process requirements.

Implementation Guide

The following implementation guide applies for systems running CFIN on S4HANA CORE releases 107 and 108 and above (up to specific support package levels).

Prerequisite: Download the Root CA and CPI certificates from the SAP-Note 3531918 attachments.

1. Upload SSL Certificates (Transaction: STRUST)

a) Go to the transaction STRUST.

b) Upload the certificates into your designated transaction STRUST under new PSE folder (e.g., SSL client ISM_S4HANA_ISMS4_DQ).

c) Save your changes.

2. Configure RFC Connection (Transaction: SM59)

a) Create a new HTTP RFC destination - ISM_API_CONNECT

b) Select connection type G (HTTP to External Server).

c) Under Technical Settings:

  • Enter the target host: sapit-dataandintegration2-prod.it-cpi018-rt.cfapps.eu10-003.hana.ondemand.com.
  • Enter the path prefix: /http/GenericInboundHttpRequest?Pid=S4HANA_ISM_IncidentSol-MatchingService.
  • Enter the port number: 443.

d) Under Logon & Security, set up SSL and authentication using your STRUST setup.

3. Test the Connection

a) To validate connectivity, use the Connection Test button in SM59

4. Activate Required BAdI

a) Go to the CFINIMG node.

Ensure the BAdI is already active from this node, if not active, Activate it

b) Select Central Finance: Target System Settings and BAdIs Central Finance.

c) In the BADI list, select Activate Error Resolution Support Content.

d) Select the implementation class FINS_CFIN_AIF_CUSTOM_FUNCTION and check for activation

 

Why This Matters

This integration is more than a technical enhancement. By automating manual processes, it reduces resolution time and improves user experience. For large enterprises, this is a significant advantage in managing complex landscapes and high-volume transactions.

Incorporating AIF and ISM into your Central Finance system is a step towards a more efficient and user-friendly error resolution process. Embrace this innovation and watch your finance operations thrive.

Keywords:

Central Finance, AIF, ISM, SAP S/4HANA, RFC, Error Handling, Automation, STRUST, SM59, BADI

entry.title: Re: Unable to find deposit payment processing capability...
entry.content: Hi @andre_nakratzas, the first approach which is DO01 and DO02 contract type, i don't think it is available in SAP Public Cloud, unless there is any scope items need to be activated? For the second approach that you mentioned by using condition type, how it will link to special G/L indicator? The requirement is to post deposit via CLM module with special G/L indicator.

entry.title: GR 相关配置问题咨询
entry.content:
1、配置ID102660,允许组货币作为主要货币是什么意思，其他系统有“科目集成的正确物料组”


2、配置DIGITS，重分类方法中的触发器要怎么配置


3、如果我需要一个以人民币出具的合并报表是不是需要新增一个组货币为CNY的合并分类账


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GR 相关配置问题咨询
entry.content:
1、配置ID102660,允许组货币作为主要货币是什么意思，

This indicator specifies if it is possible to choose the group currency as the leading currency for selected consolidation units.

  • If you set this indicator, you can decide if the local or the group currency is used as the leading currency during data collection from accounting.
  • If you don't set this indicator, the local currency is automatically used as the leading currency during data collection.

Help: 

Group Currency is Leading Currency: You can specify if the group currency should be used as the leading currency instead of the local currency in data collection. Selecting this option has the following implications 

2、配置101011，重分类方法中的触发器要怎么配置

触发器就是一个选择集合， selection. 可以在Define Selection 定义修改。

Trigger selection 包含的所有item就会被之后的 重分类程序运行。

最好使用标准的，不要太多自定义。 特别是方法不要自定义。

如果只是想修改触发集合， 你直接换个新的集合就行。新建和修改的app都是Define Selection。

3、如果我需要一个以人民币出具的合并报表是不是需要新增一个组货币为CNY的合并分类账.

可以新建一个extension version, Group currency =CNY

More information can be found in  Multiple Group Currencies

entry.title: Re: GRC QA cannot startup after DB Refresh
entry.content:
Hi, looks like the user and/or password maintained in the ABAP SSFS is not matching with the password saved within the database. Validate if the schema owner is really SAPSR3.

Check if changing the password via BR*Tools helps to eliminate this issue with commands below:

brconnect -u / -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <password> -s abap

or

brconnect -u system/<sys_pwd> -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <sap_pwd> -s abap -RDB

In case BRT$ADM user for executing BR*Tools command has been set up, enhance the command with another "/" in the command line, as seen below:

brconnect -u // -c -f chpass -o SAPSR3 -p <password> -s abap

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Professional Certification Level: What it means for customers and consultants
entry.content: I and some other colleagues suddenly found that our Professional Certificates have expired on March 31, 2025, although we take our deltas and our Associate Certificates were always valid. Did we miss something or is SAP discontinuing the concept of Professional Certificates for SuccessFactors?

entry.title: Re: Become Part of the SAP TechEd Family as a Community Talk Speaker!
entry.content: Hi @hardyp180 AI is definitely a huge piece of the puzzle, but not the only one! Please feel free to submit the topics you are passionate about and want to share with the community.

entry.title: Transitioning from SAP Design Studio to Advanced Reporting Solutions
entry.content:
The landscape of business intelligence is transforming, and staying ahead means embracing change. With the deprecation of SAP Design Studio, businesses should transition their custom reports to more advanced technologies to ensure seamless data-driven decision-making and meet emerging business needs.

Understanding the Current Situation

SAP Design Studio applications were officially deprecated in the CE2102 release and are no longer available as of CE2108. As this technology is no longer supported, it's crucial for users to explore alternative solutions to recreate their reports. Continuing with Design Studio may lead to operational risks and hinder future innovation. Despite the ability to use custom Design Studio apps, it's strongly advisable to transition to newer technologies that promise support and advancement.

Customer Options for Rebuilding Reports

To support this transition, there are several effective options for rebuilding reports:

  • Rebuild Reports Using Web Dynpro
  • Rebuild Reports Using MDA
  • Rebuild Reports Using Review Booklet

Understanding the purpose and layout can refine your choice:

Web Dynpro

  • Purpose: Used for detailed, flexible analysis and visualization of large data sets.
  • Features: Allow users to switch between grid and graphical displays, with configurable settings.

 

Multidimensional Reports

  • Purpose: Designed for detailed analysis and visualization
  • Features: Enables analysis across multiple dimensions using pivot tables and charts, with functions for slicing, dicing, and saving personal data views

 

Review Booklet

  • Purpose: Offers an aggregated data overview
  • Features: Predefined business pages that focus on presenting key insights

 

Select the appropriate report type according to your specific business requirements.

Exploring the Rebuild Path

Crafting a successful transition involves mapping the path from Design Studio to your chosen method.

  • Rebuild Reports Using Web Dynpro

Web Dynpro provides a straightforward solution for report rebuilding. Customers can easily create web Dynpro reports by creating application in the APP View Browser. Detailed steps can be checked in the demo below or the blog.

DEMO

It offers simplicity and ease of use, making it ideal for those seeking minimal time investment and consultant involvement. However, it's important to note that Web Dynpro does not feature upcoming enhancements or updates, which may influence long-term capability.

  • Rebuild Reports Using MDA

MDA emerges as a compelling option with moderate complexity, promising regular updates and enhancements.

Before creating an MDA report, queries must be recreated in the APP Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

DEMO

Following this, create the MDA report in the APP Manage KPIs and Reports with a few steps—demonstrated in the demo.

DEMO

In a word, As the successor to Web Dynpro, MDA involves initial query recreation, yet balances time, cost, and the need to remain aligned with current trends and capabilities.

  • Rebuild Reports Using Review Booklet

The Review Booklet offers a comprehensive method for report reconstruction, characterized by higher complexity and the necessity to redesign report layouts.

Firstly, similar to MDA, customers need to recreate a query in the new APP Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

DEMO

Then Navigate Review booklet tab in the App Manage KPIs and Reports to create to create a new review booklet.

As the purpose and layout of review booklet is different with multidimension report, customers need to redesign the report according to business needs.

Creating a Review Booklet in the Review Booklet Designer

While this demands significant time and consultant involvement, it assures regular updates, providing a future-proof solution for businesses with complex reporting needs.

Key Takeaway: Web Dynpro vs. MDA vs. Review Booklet

Each rebuilding strategy carries unique advantages:

  • Web Dynpro stands out for its simplicity but lacks future enhancements.
  • Review Booklet offers a robust solution with ongoing updates but requires high initial complexity.
  • MDA provides a balanced approach, coupling moderate complexity with up-to-date features and regular enhancements.(Recommanded)

 

Embracing change is essential in the journey towards more reliable, innovative reporting solutions. The transition from SAP Design Studio opens doors to enhanced analytical experiences and future-proof capabilities. This change is not just necessary, but a strategic advantage, setting up businesses for continued success in a data-driven world.

entry.title: Is there any Configuration Procedure to enable Multiple Approval Flow for Test Plans
entry.content:
Hi SAP,
 
Is there any Configuration Procedure to enable Multiple Approval Flow for Test Plans in Focused Build like mentioned below.
 

Each Test Plan Needs to be approved/signed off by Multiple User before going to start testing.
After Testing, the Test completion report needs to be approved/signed off by Multiple Users.

 
Cloud you please help us here.
 
Thanks,
Nikhil
 

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Focused Build: Test Plan Management

Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Focused Build: Ass...(truncated)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there any Configuration Procedure to enable Multip...
entry.content:
Hello,

if the test plans are created from Test Plan Management tile, you can define release status schema / release status values that require digital signature. The configuration is in tx SPRO - SAP Solution Manager - Test Suite - Test Suite Preparation - Setup - Specify Release Status Values / Set Up Release Status Schema.

A signature strategy can be assigned to each release status value, but this is useful only for REVIEW* values. See Signature Strategy in Customizing (SPRO) for SAP Solution Manager under Technical Settings -> Digital Signature.
If you set a release status value with a signature strategy, it starts immediately. The persons in the signature strategy must sign to fulfill the strategy. When the strategy is fulfilled, the assigned end status value is set automatically. If the signature strategy is cancelled, the assigned cancellation status value is set automatically.

See also Application Help Test Suite - Release Status Schema at: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/fbc7b5ecf5094fe0b6a2eb966160008f/5ab1cae0f3944d5dba6cc2b4c1a25441.html?locale=en-US

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: problem connecting process automation with build app
entry.content:
You need to perform three steps in order to see your process in the Build Apps:

1. RELEASE the process - you did it

2. Select RELEASED version of your process and DEPLOY  it - you haven't mentioned this step

3. PUBLISH the DEPLOYED version in the Build Library - you did it, but for process with nonexistent trigger

If you forgot to DEPLOY your process, the process trigger does not exist, so Build Apps can't see it.

entry.title: Re: How to assign roles to Consolidation steps ?
entry.content:
Hi T,

Yes, it is possible. In the customizing (direct below authorization group) MDCIMG -> Configure Process Template -> Specify Process Template,  you can assign the authorization group to your process template. First, select the process template what you use for your consolidation for example, then in the process step there is a column (the last one I think) and there you can assign authorization group. Then, only the user who assigned this authorization group can do the action.

Best regards,

Sungmin

entry.title: Re: Standard apps add custom button in public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @nirojan_vel ,
You can find  a nice example how to extend the Manage Sales Order - Version 2 app in the Extensibility Explorer:

https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/2851

If you scroll down there you will find a Set-up Guide with a step by step description how to develop that example.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Local Mocking of External Service not working
entry.content:
I'm following the official documentation (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services) to integrate an external OData Service (API_BUSINESS_PARTER) while trying to mock it for local development. The steps I've taken:

  1. Download the edmx file from the Business Accelerator Hub
  2. import via "cds import <input_file> --as cds"
  3. Create srv/external/data/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER-A_BusinessPartner.csv and populate with example data from the docs
  4. Run the following code:
const bupa = await cds.connect.to('API_BUSINESS_PARTNER');
const { A_BusinessPartner } = bupa.entities;
const result = await bupa.run(SELECT(A_BusinessPartner).limit(100));

 In the logs I can see the following output for cds-ts watch

[cds] - mocking API_BUSINESS_PARTNER {
  impl: '../node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js',
  path: '/odata/v4/api-business-partner'
}

However, when the code above runs I'm getting the following error:

[error] - 500 > {
  code: '500',
  message: 'no such table: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER_A_BusinessPartner in:\n' +
    `SELECT json_insert(jsonb_insert('{}','$."BusinessPartner"',BusinessPartner,'$."Customer"'

 I would have expected the server to return the mock data from the csv file, but I can't get it to work. Anything I'm missing here that's not in the official documentation? I also ran "cds deploy --to sqlite" but it's not creating the table in question...

project dependencies:

"@sap/cds": "8.7.2",
"@sap/cds-dk": "8.9.4",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity": "4.0.2",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "4.0.2",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience": "4.0.2",
"@cap-js/cds-typer": "0.35.0",
"@cap-js/cds-types": "0.10.0",
"@cap-js/sqlite": "1.11.1",
"@sap/cds-dk": "8.9.4",
"@sap/cds-lsp": "8.9.1",

entry.title: Re: How to clear old tasks on SuccessFactors dashboard
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Maybe you can look into the release which allows you to remove stale tasks with the check tool:

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-release-information/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/06712342834d4dbcaefe8722a6ac148e.html

entry.title: No update of the TM master data (/SCMTMS/ZONE) when the transportation zone in the BP is changed
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

we are using the embedded SAP Transportation Management System (S/4HANA 2021 FPS03). Currently, we are facing some issues with updating the TM master data. If a user changes the transportation zone in the Business Partner, there is unfortunately no automatic update in transaction /SCMTMS/ZONE in our system. The location remains under the old transportation zone.

Is there a solution from SAP to keep the Business Partner and TM master data synchronized? Does anyone have similar problems?

Here's an example:

1) I changed the Business Partner 38994

2) Transaction /SCMTMS/ZONE

I am looking forward to your feedback.

entry.title: Re: Open Past Period
entry.content:
Hello @Charisma_fitra 

You can't do that. Once an MM period is opened, you can't go back., unless you manually manipulate DB tables content. 

Why would you want to post to May in July?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Overwrite dimension filters in SAC Excel Add-in table based on property of dimension
entry.content:
Hi All,

Currently I am facing issue override dimensions members based on filter value.

Ex:  I have product dimension and plant is attribute. User will select plant as selection from (SAP.GETDIMENSIONFILTER), based on this plant, products need to display in row axis. I am using below function with syntax and giving #VALUE error. =SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER("Table1","D_Product","Plant=&F11",0,0). cell F11 has plant value came from plant selection using SAP.GETDIMENSIONFILTER function .... Please advise any inputs.

Thanks,

Chanti

entry.title: EWM Replenishment with HU Types and Packing Step – Error during Full HU Movement to Fixed Bin
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently facing a challenge in our SAP EWM replenishment process involving handling unit types (HU types) and process-oriented storage control (POSC). I’d like to outline the scenario and would appreciate any insights or recommendations from the community.

Use Case

We are implementing a replenishment process from a pallet storage type to a carton storage type, where the destination bin is a fixed bin that only allows smaller HU types (e.g. cartons).

To facilitate this, we’ve integrated a packing workstation into the process flow via process-oriented storage control (POSC). The idea is to:

  1. Trigger replenishment to the fixed bin.

  2. Create a warehouse task from the pallet storage type.

  3. Move the HU to the packing workstation.

  4. Repack the contents into a new HU (e.g. from pallet to carton).

  5. Putaway into the fixed bin.

System Behavior

  • Partial quantity warehouse tasks (i.e. not the full HU quantity) work perfectly:
    → The system creates the warehouse task, determines the packing work center, executes the repacking step, and completes replenishment to the fixed bin.

  • Full quantity movements (i.e. complete HU) lead to an error.
    Since pallet storage types typically require full HU movements, we have enabled rounding at storage type or warehouse process type level, so the system always tries to move the entire HU.

Issue

When the rounding results in a full HU removal, the warehouse task creation fails in transaction /SCWM/REPL with the following error:

/SCWM/PUT_BIN_DET055 - HU Type of the source HU is not allowed for the destination storage type.

 

It seems that during task creation, the system already validates the HU type against the final destination storage type (the fixed bin), even though the HU is not intended to reach the final bin in its original form – it should first go to the packing step, where it is repacked into an allowed HU type.

Question to the Community

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or knows how to handle HU type validation in replenishment scenarios involving POSC and full HU removal?

  • Is it possible to suppress the HU type check against the final bin during the initial WT creation if a packing step is part of the process?

  • Are we missing a configuration aspect in POSC, storage process definition, or warehouse process type?

  • How do you usually design replenishment with repacking via POSC, especially when dealing with full HUs and fixed bin restrictions?

Any ideas or best practices are highly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Preferential Origin – For externally procured configu...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Since you purchase these goods as finished products or semifinished goods, don't you receive a supplier LTSD?

In theory there should be no determination/calculation executed for this particular scenario as you do not care the individual status of the components. You get your preferential status for the entire finished product while your supplier is in charge to provide you such information. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content: Please don't format your post as source code. It's awkward to read.

entry.title: Re: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content: That specifies valuation classes per material type, but not per plant.

entry.title: Re: Configure Relative Dates in Manage Route Maps for a 3...
entry.content:
Hi @MollyN 

A possible option is to set a number for the due date only. Based on Form Creation Date on the first step, and then on the following steps you can use as well Form Creation Date (so if step 2 is +7 days from step 1, then step 2 would have 14 days after Form Creation Date, and so on).  Or you can use the Previous Step Due Date as a reference to determine Step Due Date for the current step.

NOTE: In order to be able to save the changes, you will need to select an option for every date, even though it has to be left blank. I am adding a screenshot of what I am referring to, with the error and without the error. 

KBA that might be helpful as well 

2087333 - Route Maps - Start Date, Due Date, Exit Date, Enforce Start Date, and Automatic send on due date Validation - Performance Management https://me.sap.com/notes/2087333/E

Best is to test it and see if you get the expected results. 

No error

I m on Premise 2023 FPS00 and I want to use the standard API API_CONDITION_CONTRACT. Unfortunately no extensibility is supported as specified in the documentation. I need to add custom fields to the API . Ideally I would like also to add the standard structure WCOCOI which is not yet supported but it is a component of the condition contract. 

What is the recommendation to add the custom fields when no extensibility is available?

  • Copy the standard API and add the custom fields?
  • Enhance the standard CDS views and use implicit enhancement in the Behavior Implementation?
  • Create our own API 

@Andre_Fischer maybe you can help.

Thank you

Christophe

Message no. VLA319"

OBD created with One line item with 2 kg

PGI created with split batch 001 1kg and batch 002 1kg

Background Inbound delivery is created

GRN has been posted with respect to Inbound delivery.

Kindly suggest any solution.

entry.title: Re: Open Past Period
entry.content: Hi Dominik, We have several data that we haven't uploaded yet

entry.title: Regex Filtering in File Name for SFTP Sender Adapter in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
SAP has quietly introduced Regex Filtering in the SFTP Sender Adapter, starting with adapter version 1.17 for SFTP Sender and version 1.18 for SFTP Poll Enrich.
Previously, although SAP documentation claimed regex support, it was not truly evaluated by the runtime. Only simple expressions using * and ? were interpreted:
* - matches zero or more characters
? - matches exactly one character

For example:
ab* matches: ab, abc, abcd
ab? matches: abc, ab1
This limitation created a challenge in SAP Process Orchestration (PO) to SAP Cloud Integration (CI) migration projects, where regex filtering in PO SFTP channels was already supported.

From SFTP adapter version 1.17 onward, a new checkbox - Regex Filtering - is available. Once enabled, the file name input field can evaluate actual regular expressions, bringing it in line with PO capabilities.
For example, to pick up files named abc.txt or 123.txt, you can now use:
^(abc\.txt|123\.txt)$

Old SFTP adapterNew SFTP adapter

 

 

If you are not Seeing the 'Regex Filtering' Option, it likely means you are using an older adapter version.
Solution: Delete the existing SFTP channel and create a new one using the latest adapter version.

FYI...You can take AI (ChatGPT, Copilot) help to come up with regex you need and use it in SFTP channel.

entry.title: Report Similar To IP19 & IP24 For Unscheduled Maintenance Plan
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we need a report similar to IP24 for unscheduled maintenance plans with 

1. Order type

2. Plant

3. Planner group

4. Scheduled/unscheduled

5. start date

6. call horizon

7. Scheduling indicator for MPLAN

8. scheduling period

9. cycle

10. unit

11. Total calls to be generated from all plans - tilldate- Start date> Cycle > call horizon> scheduling period

12. Total calls to be generated from all plans - till scheduling period end - Start date> Cycle > call horizon> scheduling period

13. Total order generated till date - Start date> Cycle > call horizon> scheduling period

14. Total order generated till scheduling period's end - Start date> Cycle > call horizon> scheduling period

User want REPORT similar to IP19 & IP24 for forecasting orders(calls) that will be generated from unscheduled MPLANS before scheduling .How we can develop the report through tables MHIO MHIS MPLA etc , also need logic for scheduling indicator changes & impact over future calls.

Thanks In Advance.

Ali Shehpar

entry.title: Re: Credit exposure commitment type 500 is not updated wh...
entry.content:
Hi,

thanks for reply.

In my opinion the reason is that standard credit exposure update logic based only on deliveries or on orders created in ERP system
We are using external TMS from SAP, integrated with our S4, but seems that this integration not always cover standard functionality.

When SD invoice is created from Settlement document credit exposure is updated correctly for commitment type 100 (open order value is updated) but othing happend on the other side - I expected to see new record with billing document value on commitment 500 for open billing documents but it is not happend.

When invoice is posted -> commitment type 200 is again updated correctly and value of the invoice appears as OPEN FI ITEM.

So for me it seems to be a GAP in SAP logic which does not consider that commitment type 500 can be updated as well by external transaction (from integrated system)

Answering your other hints: BADI_SD_CM is activated and it is call correctly, nothing is in the queues

entry.title: BPC SAP UI Landscape Configuration is missing
entry.content:
Dear guys,

we were faced with a sudden problem using SAP BPC in Analysis-for-Office: Trying to open BPC fails due to "SAPUI andscape Configuration is not available". And actually I do not find the place where it is missing and where to add it. It has worked in the past. What I saw: the AddIn EPM is missing on the clients. Could that be the reason? I am not familiar with the configuration and setup of BPC. So has anyone any idea how to fix it? As were are in the mid of planning process there is management awareness to it. Thanks for your help. Regards, Peter

Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a Flexible workflow for sales credit memo requests. For that, I am trying to assign a custom field(approval reason id) to manage teams and responsibility. I have added that in the FIORI app but I am unable to do the same in the config step named "Assign Custom Responsibility Definition to CDS View". I am getting the following error: " For unreleased CDS views in SAP namespace, only custom fields are allowed". I believe because of this error I am not able to successfully complete the steps.

I'm not sure which process i need to follow if you are aware of any solution could you please let me know.

I need a custom responsibility definition under responsibility section while creating  team. 

I followed  below articles but still not able to find the exact solution.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/30fa795560884d96812e937cd90f6165.html

entry.title: Re: How to do a cube to cube join in Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hi,

it is only possible to join two views/tables but no analytic model. You can stack Analytic Models to reuse them. If you need the information in one view, you can have a look at this blog post https://www.nextlytics.com/blog/test-automation-with-sql-in-sap-datasphere

The idea is to flatten an Analytic Model and create a database view.

entry.title: Re: Set a default hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi Andrea,

currently, there is no default for a dimension display. It starts with the flat list. Then select the Hierarchy and choose your desired hierarchy. 

This is only when you start from scratch for a SAC story. When you already have your AM added to a story, you can define the behavior. 

But you are right, I am also still waiting for this option 😉

I feel, it could be due to some technical issue it is displayed and should be removed.  Can someone please confirm this

cc @KatherineKenneally

entry.title: Re: SAP CRM – Scope Filter Not Working Until Page Refresh...
entry.content:
I'm afraid there is no simple/obvious answer. You can try the following steps, maybe it helps in identifying the problem:

  • Go to Transaction SU3 and set the user parameter WCF_IGNORE_ENHANCEMT = 'A'. This will deactivate all your Z-code. If the problem disappears, you can be sure that it is caused by your custom implementation.
  • Open the Component in BSP_WD_CMPWB and find out what happens during the page refresh. You can set breakpoints in DO_PREPARE_OUTPUT of your _IMPL class, or in ON_NEW_FOCUS of the _CNxx class. Check whether you can identify a difference. For a user that doesn't have this problem, what is different? Trace your error back to where the context node is filled. If it is a search object, debug the search object. Maybe there is some BAdI implementation, some authorization check, or something else that prevents the data from being read.

entry.title: Re: FAGLL03H : How to export a lot of G/L postings to Excel without facing time out issues?
entry.content:

lieben Dank für diesen Beitrag. Ich stelle mir nur die Frage, wie ich vorgehen muß, wenn ich nicht die Belegsummen sondern die Einzelposten aus den Belegen exportieren möchte. Gibt es da auch einen Weg?

Lieben Dank und Grüße

Petra Spieß

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 BY-PRODUCT
entry.content:
Hello,
I created my normal items, and I created the BOM for only the main item. The sub-product was declared in the BOM with a standard cost, and the cost of the main product will be calculated using the BOM.
The problem I encountered is that even though I separated the items by store, the cost of my main item doesn't include the cost of the sub-product.
Explanation: Item costs = item BOM cost + sub-product cost.
My goal is to have the pure item cost without the cost of the sub-product.

entry.title: Automatic conversion of quantities-unit of measure on stock display
entry.content:
Hello, hope this message finds you well.


My question is about Warehouse Management on the Cloud Public.
I have set alternative uom on the product 43´s masterdata. 1 bag = 10 pc.


But the stock is shown in the warehouse monitor with in the uom pc both in the regular and in the alternative one.


I have attached the print screens. 


I need for the system to automatically display both quantities (in base-uom and converted in alternative uom). Example: base uom 100 pc; alternative uom 10 bag. 


May you help me with that?


Best Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Automatic conversion of quantities-unit of measure on...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

If you want to display one of the stock line with BUoM and Alt. UoM for physical stock, Please go to APP Warehouse Monitor -> Physical Stock, Select the stock line -> Click More method -> Change Alternative UoM.

 

But if you want the stock to be putaway into the bin with BAG as Alt.UoM automatically when the stock is moved into the bin. Please follow below steps:

  1. Move all the stock out of the storage type and leave one stock entry, convert the Alt.UoM as BAG.
  2. Stet the "QuantAddnStk AUoM" set as "First Alternative UoM Dominant" and "Level of Avail. Qty" can be set as "Storage Bin" at Customizing "Maintain Storage Types".
  3. Move the rest of the stock back.

KBA 3616708 Customizing "Maintain Storage Types"-> "QuantAddnStk AUoM" set as "First Alternative UoM Dominant" doesn't work - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News June 2025
entry.content:
It's amazing to see how CALM has evolved over the last couple of years and it is really good that you keep spreading news about the latest and greatest.

One suggestion would be to cover the libraries in more detail. They are all available now, but the guidance and documentation in this area is lacking behind the "normal" CALM standard 😉

I don't think is sth on the sql query, i guess it's related to the Layout settings. I have tried to change this in PRD:

But nothing happens. Also tried to bind that cell directly to the FNAME and LNAME of the EnrollmentInfo_Child data set but then it shows as empty when running it in the instance.

Any ideas suggestions to achieve that display of FNAME LNAME?

Thanks!

Cris

Is it possible to achieve both the goods receipt and the reduction of O inventory quantity through an API interface for external collaboration procurement?

entry.title: Button handler not called in adaptation
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

I try to extend a standard App "Create maintenance request" (F1511A). I have added a Fragment with button and the button is available:

My Fragment look like this:

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core='sap.ui.core' xmlns='sap.m'>
    <Button id='LorCapBtn' text="LorCap" press='extension.customer.create.notif.variant.onLorCapPress'/> 
</core:FragmentDefinition>

The controller:

sap.ui.define(
    [
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension'
        ,'sap/m/MessageBox'
        // ,'sap/ui/core/mvc/OverrideExecution'
    ],
    function (
        ControllerExtension
        ,MessageBox
        // ,OverrideExecution
    ) {
        'use strict';
        return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.create.notif.variant.MainCntrlExt", {
            onLorCapPress : function(oEvent){
                console.log("onLorCapPress called");
                MessageBox.success("Hello");
            },
});
    }
);

When I press the button nothing happened (no massege box, no message in console).

Do you know what is the problem? What have to be done to run my code?

Regards,

Boguslaw

entry.title: Re: 生产订单联产品结算规则必填
entry.content:
你好，在生产订单中，结算规则为必填项，且无法为空，但可以通过调整结算规则的方法来支持这种特殊情况下的成本分摊。例如，使用特定的成本分配方法来处理未产出的联产品成本。

  1. 利用不同的结算规则和等值数：

    • 使用特定的结算规则（如PP5或PP6）分配生产成本，这些规则允许用计划产出数量或实际交货数量来设置等值数，从而适应生产过程中联产品未产出的情况，参考链接：联产品制造中的结算规则 | SAP Help Portal

  2. 使用事件驱动的生产会计：

    • 在事件驱动的生产会计中，可以为非固定价格的联产品使用分摊法进行成本分配，该方法基于订单头维护的分摊规则，参考链接：基于事件的成本分配（联产品和副产品） | SAP Help Portal。

从业务场景和联产品的业务定义上看，若业务流程流转到制造执行过程中，生产因为临时工艺调整导致最终的产成品发生变化（如最终产成品不等同于生产订单抬头物料）。理论上应该关闭当前生产订单，建立新的生产订单。

其次，如果此类情况经常发生，建议客户可以从业务重要性重新评估是否需要将该产品定义为联产品。并且重新考虑该产品对于主产品的成本影响和占比，以及是否需要将其改定义为副产品。

建议联系您的咨询服务商进行业务流程背景的深入探讨寻求更为适合的解决方案。

以上信息供参考。

谢谢

entry.title: API interface for reversing goods receipt
entry.content: When reversing a goods receipt, does the system create a new document with movement type 102? If so, how to reverse the original document? Is it possible to perform this reversal operation through an API interface?

entry.title: Q2 2025: Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
In the second quarter of 2025, we released several new features and enhancements. Did you know that you can find all new feature announcements for SAP Cloud Identity Services in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Release Notes on the SAP Help Portal?

Let’s take a look at some of our recent highlights!

Identity Provisioning Service: Job log improvements

The Job Execution Outcome is now displayed on the Job Execution Details screen and included in the job notifications. It appears when delta-read jobs and jobs that fail to read entities from the source system affect the deletion of entities in the target system.
For more information, see Monitor Provisioning Job Logs.

You can now download and view the details of all deleted entities by a provisioning job. To enable this, set the property ips.trace.deleted.entity to true.
For more information, see Manage Provisioning Job Logs and List of Properties.

Identity Directory Service and Identity Provisioning Service: Support for extension schemas towards many SAP business applications

We now support a number of extension schemas, including

  • SAP extension schema for groups
  • Extension enterprise schema for users
  • SAP ODM User Schema Support

You can find details in the SAP Cloud Identity Release Notes.

Extension of the Identity Directory’s SCIM API

The Identity Directory API of SAP Cloud Identity Services now returns the termsOfUse and privacyPolicy parameters. For more information, check Identity Directory API.

In addition, we now support application-specific group assignments. You can find details in the SAP Help Portal chapters on Identity Authentication and the Local Identity Directory.

Management of Corporate Identity Providers via API

Tenant administrators can manage corporate identity providers in Identity Authentication via API. It offers endpoints for CRUD operations (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) over the corporate identity providers. See SAP Cloud Identity Services Corporate Identity Providers Directory.

Configuration of Password Expiration Reminder

You can configure the system to show a password expiration reminder on the user’s sign-in page. For more information, check Enable Password Expiration Reminder.

Enabling SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI with support for Microsoft Windows mandatory profiles

We added support for single sign-on on shopfloor Windows-based computers that are configured to use a mandatory profile. This protects shopfloor computers with a restrictive mandatory profile while still offering single sign-on support for desktop clients of SAP GUI. You can find more information in the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

Cloud Foundry Login Based on JSON Web Tokens (JWTs)

When building CI/CD pipelines, developers are responsible for managing the credentials used by these pipelines for authentication to external systems. This traditionally requires developers to take care of the lifecycle of the credentials, including secure storage and regular rotation of the credentials, which comes with repeated efforts and might be error-prone.

In his latest blog post, Authenticating GitHub Actions Workflows Deploying to the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment with JWTs, Sebastian Ries explains how to authenticate to the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment from a GitHub Actions workflow using GitHub-issued short-lived JSON Web Tokens (JWTs), eliminating the need for manual secret management and enabling secure, efficient CI/CD workflows. 

Want to stay up to date on our services? Join our SAP BTP Security, Single Sign-On for SAP GUI and SAP Cloud Identity Services communities!

entry.title: API interfaces for the reversal of production goods receipt and production material issue
entry.content: For the reversal of production goods receipt and production material issue, is it necessary to reverse the original document and then create a new document? If so, what are the corresponding API interfaces for these operations?

entry.title: Re: EWM transfer stock from non EWM storage location to EWM storage location
entry.content: Hi Dominik, do you know maybe what to do when material document is created instead of inbound delivery? I'm also using movement type 311

entry.title: The API interface for cost-based material withdrawal
entry.content: Is “./sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/A_MaterialDocumentHeader” the correct API interface for cost-based material withdrawal? Are there any differences in the interface parameters compared to those for a standard document?

entry.title: Re: Regarding CAP Java target and custom error messages
entry.content:
There are specific annotations for input validations that create specific error messages: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#input-validation However, there are no annotations to define custom error messages for a field.

It wouldn't be enough to specify the content of the error message, but you'd also need to specify when to throw these. You have to do this programmatically in an event handler and use the message target API to indicate the elements targeted by your error.

See https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/event-handlers/indicating-errors#target for more details.

entry.title: Embracing AI in Field Service: From Garden Tools to Smart Assistance
entry.content:
(Blog 2 of the “Transformation in Field Service” series)

When we first added a smart irrigation system to our garden, I was skeptical. Could a sensor and some automation really replace the watchful care of a gardener? It didn’t. But it did make us smarter. We still prune, plant, and pick. But now we get alerts before things dry out, insights about soil health, and more time for what really matters: growing beautiful things.

That, to me, is what AI should feel like in Field Service.

Why AI Is Top of Mind

AI is no longer a futuristic buzzword. With the rise of Generative AI and machine learning, it's becoming a real part of how organizations operate. For Field Service companies, transformation is top of mind—and not without reason.

Service teams face serious pressure:

  • Customers expect faster response and personalized service

  • Skilled labor is increasingly scarce

  • Costs are rising, while expectations keep growing

AI offers a way to cope. But here’s the catch: it only helps if it fits the people who need to use it.

Field Service: A Surprising AI Frontrunner?

You might assume AI will take hold first in data-driven industries like finance or marketing. But Field Service might just beat them to it.

Why? Because AI in Field Service solves exactly the things many technicians don’t want to deal with. These are hands-on experts who often chose their profession precisely to avoid sitting in front of software.

If AI can:

  • Remove repetitive tasks

  • Reduce time spent on documentation

  • Automate job filtering and planning

  • Provide guidance without asking questions

…then it becomes not just useful—but welcome. And that’s where adoption begins.

AI Doesn’t Replace People—It Supports Them

AI in Field Service doesn’t replace know-how, experience, or context. It supports the humans doing the work—giving them tools to focus on what they do best.

  • Dispatchers get intelligent support to make faster, better-informed scheduling decisions

  • Technicians get equipment insights, task summaries, and job predictions—without needing to dig through software

  • Managers gain better visibility into performance and planning scenarios

It’s not about handing over control—it’s about working smarter.

What’s Already Available in SAP Field Service Management

SAP Field Service Management already includes a growing suite of AI features designed to make life easier for dispatchers and technicians. Here are a few highlights:

  • Joule for Dispatchers (Doc, Video)
    Boost efficiency and get quick access to help documentation with a natural language assistant.

  • Intelligent Filtering (Doc, Video)
    Use natural language to search and filter jobs more intuitively and accurately.

  • Equipment Insights (Doc, Video)
    Get smart summaries of past jobs and proactive equipment monitoring.

  • Activity Summary (Doc, Video)
    See how similar issues were resolved—improving repair planning and speed.

  • Predictive Routing (Doc, Video)
    Minimize travel time by finding the most efficient route.

  • Job Duration Prediction (Doc, Video)
    Estimate how long a job will take using Machine Learning.

  • AI Policy Designer & Auto-scheduling (Doc, Video)
    Set up rules and simulate auto-scheduling tailored to your business logic.

  • Planning Simulations (Doc, Video)
    Visualize the impact of scheduling choices before you commit.

Each of these is designed not to replace humans—but to take work off their plate.

Embracing AI Thoughtfully

If you want your AI initiatives to succeed, treat them like a new team member:

  • Explain what it does—and what it doesn’t

  • Make its logic transparent and explainable

  • Start small, get feedback, and improve

  • Bring people along for the journey

Trust and adoption go hand in hand. Don’t expect people to embrace what they don’t understand.

Back to the Garden

AI won’t plant your seeds. It won’t know which flowers matter most to you. But it will remind you to water them, help you spot what’s missing, and give you more time to enjoy what you’ve grown.

The same is true in Field Service. Embracing AI isn’t about replacing people. It’s about helping them flourish—on their own terms.

Intro to the Series:

👉The Heart of Transformation: People, Process, and Persistence

SAP Field Service Management

entry.title: SAP ALM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

1) Here i"m integrating with SAPALM to Fresh service.

WE I"m getting below error in Mapping Rules, Do we have some standard mapping for Fresh service.


Error Details ::

422 Unprocessable Entity on POST request for "https://x-rn-srv/message": "{"errorDetails":{"code":"422 UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY","message":"Configuration error occurred: Bad Request","target":"/message","details":[]}}".


2) Here i"m integrating with SAP ALM to BTP IS.

we i"m facing with connection issue, partner is not assigned to SAPALM service. I tried to configure the service from SAPALM partner is not visible.

Need to setup the connection between SAPALM and BTP IS..

details ::

Log on to SAP BTP Cockpit and navigate to the sub-account for your SAP Build Process Automation 

  1. Go to Connectivity > Destinations
  2. Click on "New Destination"
  3. Create a new destination using the "Blank Template" view
    1. Name: Enter a name for the destination as sap_process_automation_cloud_alm
    2. Type: HTTP
    3. URL: SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter "Endpoints" > "Api" without api (make sure to keep the / at the end)
    4. Authentication: Select 'OAuth2ClientCredentials'
    5. Client ID: SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter "clientid"
    6. Client Secret: SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter "clientsecret"
    7. Token Service URL: SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter "uaa" > "url" + /oauth/token


Required connection setup URL's to specify..

entry.title: Re: Full name of the Personnel number?
entry.content:
nice Thanks ,

Franck

entry.title: Understanding Identity and Access Management (IAM) in your SAP Landscape
entry.content:
As companies adopt cloud services, hybrid infrastructures, and mobile workforces, traditional security models no longer hold up. That’s where Identity and Access Management (IAM) steps in as a foundational element of modern cybersecurity strategies.

 

What is IAM?

IAM, or Identity and Access Management, refers to the set of processes, technologies, and policies that ensure the right individuals—employees, partners, customers—have access to the right resources at the right times for the right reasons. It encompasses how users are authenticated, how their access is granted or revoked, and how their digital identities are managed throughout their lifecycle.

In short, IAM is about answering two key questions:

  • Who are you?
  • What are you allowed to do in the system landscape?

 

Properly implemented IAM safeguards critical systems and data from unauthorized access, minimizes risk, and supports regulatory compliance. It’s a key enabler for digital transformation!

 

IAM in the Context of the Secure Operations Map (SOM)

In SAP’s Secure Operations Map (SOM), IAM falls under the User & Identity Management domain. This domain addresses how user identities are created, maintained, and authenticated across enterprise systems. It’s closely tied to both operational efficiency and the ability to secure access in a consistent, auditable way.

This positioning reflects IAM’s importance not just as a security function, but as a business enabler. IAM is a central element for maintaining secure, scalable operations across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

 

IAM in the Context of the Secure Operations Map (SOM)

Why is IAM So Crucial Today?

IAM has evolved in response to the shifting landscape of IT security. To understand its current importance, it helps to contrast legacy models with today’s Zero Trust approach.

 

Legacy Security Model: Perimeter-Based Thinking

Traditionally, enterprise security was focused on protecting the network perimeter. Tools like firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and VPNs were used to keep threats out. The assumption was that anything inside the network could be trusted.

However, this model has several flaws:

  • Insider threats remain unmitigated.
  • It doesn’t scale well to cloud or remote access.
  • Once attackers breach the perimeter, they can move laterally with little resistance.

 

Zero Trust Security Model: Trust Nothing by Default

In contrast, Zero Trust assumes breach as a baseline. It operates on the principle of “never trust, always verify.” In this model:

  • Every user and device must authenticate continuously.
  • Access is granted based on identity, context, and least privilege.
  • Identity becomes the new security perimeter.

This is why modern IAM is more than just a directory or a login page—it's the core of Zero Trust architecture.

Identity becomes the new security perimeter

SAP Cloud Identity Services (SCI): Your IAM Gateway

Within the SAP ecosystem, the primary tool for IAM is SAP Cloud Identity Services (SCI). SCI is not just a technical necessity—it’s a strategic enabler that helps organizations innovate faster and more securely.

 

What is SAP Cloud Identity Services?

SAP Cloud Identity Services is a suite of cloud-based solutions designed to provide seamless, secure, and scalable identity management across SAP and third-party applications. Its key components are:

  • Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
    Handles user login and Single Sign-On (SSO), with support for modern authentication protocols like SAML, OAuth, and OpenID Connect.
  • Identity Provisioning Service (IPS)
    Automates the creation, update, and removal of user accounts across connected systems. Reduces manual effort and risk of human error.
  • Identity Directory
    Stores user attributes and acts as a central repository for identity data, enabling fine-grained control over access.
  • Authorization Management

Helps to define and manage fine-grained access policies for SAP BTP applications based on user or business object attributes. Assign policies using group management in the identity directory, either via SAP Cloud Identity Services UI or the SCIM API.

 

Together, these services allow organizations to unify identity management across cloud, hybrid, and on-premise systems—accelerating time-to-value while improving compliance and security.

SAP IAM integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services

How to Get Started with IAM in SAP?

If you're new to IAM or just beginning your journey with SAP Cloud Identity Services, here’s a roadmap to guide you:

  • Explore the IAM Reference Architectures
    Understand the big picture by reviewing SAP’s recommended architectures for IAM: IAM Reference Architectures
  • Get Hands-On with Tutorials
    Try out SCI in a test environment to see how provisioning works in practice. You can also start with a mission on SAP discovery center https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3116/3152/
  • Collaborate Across Teams
    IAM is not a siloed effort. Talk to your Identity & Access Management colleagues and align on strategies for integrating supported systems. Supported Systems in SCI
  • Involve Experts Early
    IAM often reaches beyond the SAP center of competence into enterprise architecture, HR systems, compliance, and even customer experience. Bring in experts who can help you design processes that are secure, user-friendly, and scalable.

 

Final Thoughts

Implementing IAM is not just about ticking a compliance box or improving login flows. It’s about protecting your organization’s most valuable assets—its data, systems, and people—while enabling agility and innovation.

IAM is a journey, not a one-time project. As you build your foundation with SAP Cloud Identity Services, keep in mind that success depends on both technical integration and cross-functional collaboration.

The important IAM domain of secure authentication will be covered in my next post, where I’ll  share practical tips on how to ensure secure connection works both in cloud and on premises.

entry.title: Extending drc report for Hungary
entry.content:
My requirement is to implement drc solution for audit report.

It requires to be extended to fulfil company specific requirements.

Is there anyone who can help me on the development/extension of DRC report and it's configuration

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Composite Roles in launchpad content and layout tools
entry.content:
Hello @K4I,

is the space part of the role menu of the composite role? If yes, could you try to delete it from there and then create a single role only containing the space and adding this single role to the already existing composite role? This is the way Jocelyn describes in the "Exemplary composite role structure with shared space..." illustration. Does it work then? You could also check after this, if it still works if you derive the "space-only" role and add this instead of the master "space-only" role to the composite role.

If not, maybe the following SAP note will help, describing an issue with derived roles not being fully supported in the Manage Launchpad Pages application: 3625412 - Error "Out of role context" when editing launchpad pages with composite roles in SAP Fiori Launchpad

Best regards
Simon

entry.title: Re: For the intercompany transactions, is it possible to...
entry.content: Perform search at SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public - Business Accelerator Hub.

entry.title: Re: 依据生产订单预留创建领料申请
entry.content:
你好，生产订单在生成时系统自动产生相应领料预留。 而在 SAP S/4HANA 公有云中，目前没有一个直接支持从生产订单中依据生产订单数量创建领料预留并与实际领料数量相一致的标准功能，系统提供了一步法的组件领料和过账功能，但不支持两步骤分开申请和确认的过程，参照APP“pick component for production order”。SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

如果此业务需求对于客户的业务流程起关键性影响，建议联系您的客户/伙伴服务经理，提交相关功能改进需求。如WCR或者CIP。

谢谢。
